Ad Text:
Who we are?

On any given day, 2.5 billion people globally use Unilever products to feel good, look good and get more out of life. Many of our brands are well-known, household staples and old-time favourites including Dove, Rexona, Lynx, Vaseline, OMO/Persil, Surf, TRESemmé, and Continental. 


We have a long tradition of being a progressive, responsible business. It goes back to the days of our founder William Lever, who launched the world’s first purposeful brand, Sunlight Soap more than 100 years ago, and it’s at the heart of how we run our company today. In 2022 Unilever Australia & New Zealand became a Certified B Corporation™ (B Corp), joining a growing network of organisations committed to galvanising a stronger, more inclusive, equitable and regenerative economy for all.  



A job at Unilever is a career made by you, with development opportunities, benefits and a working culture that embraces diversity and is pioneering flexibility. There’s no better time to join our team! 



Job Description Overview

The Unilever Tatura factory produces Foods products for Australia and New Zealand, focusing on manufacturing efficiency and cost management.


Reporting to the Head of Supply Chain Finance, the Factory Finance Business Partner oversees cost control, savings, and financial stewardship within the supply chain.


This position works closely with leadership teams across the factory and Foods business, requiring independence, resilience, and strong conflict management to achieve key results.



Main Responsibilities

Finance Lead in the Tatura Factory on performance management, risk governance, key strategic planning, and accountability

Drive end-to-end Supply Chain Finance agenda for Tatura Factory and Foods Business Unit to enable profitable growth

Provide analysis and actionable insights to the Foods Business Unit Leadership Team, Tatura Factory Leadership Team and ANZ SC Finance Team

Key member of the ANZ SC Finance team, who contributes to overall ANZ Finance and ANZ SCF agenda, including stewardship and process simplification

Cross-Functional collaboration with the Production, Engineering, Procurement and R&D team on new product development, savings opportunities, capex investments, and other ad-hoc projects


Key Skills

Trusted Business Partner - Ability to build relationships with a wide range of people

Analytical Capability identifying actions and insights  

Performance Management and holding others to account

Experience of working in a changing environment, building processes

Leading and building Teams


Key Competencies

Firm & self-confident

Bias for Speed, Accuracy & Action

Strategic thinker – connect the dots

Eye for detail

Customer Focus


Other critical characteristics

High levels of drive & determination to deliver tangible results improvement

Excellent communication and relationship building skills

Ability to work with cross functional team environment

Ability to manage competing priorities

Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills


To foster effective collaboration between the Head Office and Factory site, this position involves quarterly travel. Candidates located in Sydney will be expected to travel to Tatura, while those based in Tatura will be required to travel to Sydney (CBD and North Rocks office).


Why Join Us? 

At Unilever, 94% of our employees at the Tatura factory would recommend us to friends and family. Here’s why: 

On-site Support: Access to a social worker, and soft tissue clinic offering physiotherapy services

Financial Rewards: pay reviews, and generous service awards

Supplementary Sick Leave and Insurance Coverage: Ability to apply for additional paid sick leave for up to 76 calendar days, and salary continuance insurance for up to 2 years

Inclusive Culture: Support for gender affirmation, interchangeable leave, career breaks, and domestic and family violence leave

Flexible Work: Flexible working policy and 16 weeks of paid parental leave

Product Perks: Company parcels packed with Unilever products each month

Recognition Programs: Peer reward and recognition initiatives. 

Exclusive Discounts: Deals with top brands like Dell, Apple, Headspace, HSBC Bank, AON Insurance, Bupa, Dermalogica, and more

Career Development: Access to training through Degreed, Udemy, LinkedIn Learning, and study support. 


This is more than a job—it’s a chance to be part of a team that values innovation, safety, and continuous improvement. You’ll work in a collaborative environment where your ideas matter and your contributions make a real impact. You will also enjoy an attractive remuneration package that reflects your skills and impact. 


If you’re ready to take on the challenge and thrive in an environment where high performance meets flexibility and career progression, apply today!

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business, responsibly and at speed.

As we continue to grow, we’re doubling down on what matters most: governed, enterprise-grade AI.

We’re not just solving for better insights, we’re solving for control, accountability, and trust in AI systems that act, not just advise.

We’re looking for an AI Data Governance Lead to help shape, lead, and scale how our clients govern data and AI.

This is a senior role for someone who understands that data governance is no longer just about data, it’s about enabling safe, scalable, and auditable AI. You’ll play a critical role in helping organisations move from fragmented experimentation to industrialised, governed AI capability through structured operating models, controls, and frameworks. 

You’ll sit at the intersection of data, AI, risk, and business strategy , working closely with executives while also guiding delivery teams to embed governance into real-world systems.

This role is ideal for someone who can:

Lead from the front, shaping strategy, offerings, and client outcomes
Translate complex governance and AI risk concepts into practical execution
Build trust with senior stakeholders while driving meaningful delivery
Balance control with speed, enabling AI, not blocking it


Key Responsibilities

AI & Data Governance Leadership

Lead the design and implementation of enterprise-wide data and AI governance frameworks, aligned to regulatory, ethical, and business requirements
Define AI governance operating models, including decision rights, approval workflows, and accountability structures
Establish governance foundations required to support agentic AI and autonomous systems, including runtime controls and policy enforcement


Enterprise AI 

Help design and provide structure, governance, and delivery discipline for scaled AI adoption 
Define enterprise-wide AI operating models, standards, and governance guardrails
Enable the transition from experimentation to a governed AI workforce with visibility, control, and auditability


Policy, Risk & Compliance Integration

Translate legal, regulatory, and risk requirements into clear, enforceable governance policies and controls
Align with global standards such as NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, and emerging AI regulations 
Ensure AI systems have defensible audit trails, clear accountability, and compliance alignment


Data Foundations for AI

Define and uplift core data capabilities required for AI, including:
Data quality and observability
Metadata and lineage
Access controls and security
Data ownership and stewardship models


Runtime Governance & AI Control

Support implementation of runtime governance mechanisms (e.g. policy enforcement and access control)
Ensure safe, compliant AI execution in production environments


Stakeholder Leadership & Delivery

Act as a trusted advisor to executives across data, risk, compliance, and technology
Lead workshops, governance forums, and executive briefings
Coordinate cross-functional stakeholders to ensure alignment and delivery momentum
Mentor and guide consultants across governance, data, and AI domains


Requirements


7+ years’ experience in data governance, AI governance, risk, or data consulting
Proven experience leading governance programs across data and/or AI
Strong understanding of governance frameworks and standards (e.g. DAMA-DMBOK, NIST, ISO)
Experience designing operating models, policies, standards, and stewardship frameworks
Deep understanding of data foundations required for AI, including quality, lineage, metadata, and access control
Experience working on or alongside AI/ML or advanced analytics initiatives
Familiarity with AI governance challenges, including explainability, accountability, bias, and compliance
Exposure to governance and metadata tools (e.g. Collibra, Alation, Informatica)
Strong consulting capability , able to operate from boardroom to delivery team
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders


Benefits



We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you.

Here’s what that looks like:

Grow With Purpose

Structured development pathways so you always know what you’re working toward.
Paid certifications across all major cloud and data technologies — because mastery matters
Weekly training, guilds and knowledge exchanges where practitioners share real project insights.


Support That Helps You Thrive

A dedicated support lead to mentor, guide and help you navigate your career
Regular 360° feedback loops, giving you clear direction and honest insight into your progress.


A Community You’ll Enjoy Being Part Of

Innovation days where you can experiment, prototype and sharpen new skills.
Team days, social catchups,  many new community clubs…
Opportunities to lead, influence and shape our growing practice as we expand across the region.


If you want to be part of a team that cares just as much about how we deliver as what we deliver; where curiosity is valued, ideas matter, and your impact is felt - drop us a message and start the conversation today...

The Opportunity

We’re seeking an experienced Senior FP&A Analyst for a long-term contract with a well-known and respected organisation. This role offers an immediate start and the chance to take ownership of the FY27 budget. You’ll work closely with a collaborative, high-performing finance team and play a key role in shaping financial planning and analysis for the business.

The Role

This is a hands-on role that will see you involved in a variety of core accounting and reporting responsibilities.

Create, review, and finalise the FY27 budget model, ensuring accuracy and alignment with business objectives.
Develop and maintain financial reports, dashboards, and KPIs, ensuring data is accurate, accessible, and actionable.
Perform financial analysis, including budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis, to support strategic decision-making.
Collaborate with finance, property, operations, and other teams to gather inputs, provide insights, and communicate complex financial information to stakeholders.
Support business planning by providing actionable insights and recommendations to senior leadership.
Identify opportunities to improve financial planning, reporting processes, and overall efficiency.
About You

Preferably CA/CPA qualified.
Relevant experience within a commercial finance or FP&A role.
Strong Excel skills and experience with financial modelling.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, able to present to senior leadership.

Infrastructure & EUC Business Analyst
Adelaide | contract | Hybrid (3 days onsite)

We’re working with a major organisation undergoing a large-scale technology transformation, and they’re looking for an experienced Business Analyst with strong infrastructure and end-user computing (EUC) exposure to join the program.

This is a hands-on role where you’ll be working across workplace technology, devices, cloud and identity environments, supporting key initiatives including device rollouts, M365, network uplift and broader infrastructure changes.

You’ll be responsible for gathering and documenting requirements, running workshops, mapping current and future state environments, and working closely with technical teams, architects and stakeholders to ensure solutions are fit for purpose.

What we’re looking for:

Strong experience as a Business Analyst within infrastructure, EUC or workplace technology environments
Exposure to technologies such as Intune, Microsoft 365, Azure, device management and identity (AD/IAM)
Proven experience producing artefacts such as BRDs, user stories, process maps and workflows
Ability to engage with both technical and business stakeholders and translate requirements clearly
Experience working within large programs or transformation environments

This is a great opportunity to join a high-impact program with a lot of moving parts, where you’ll be embedded in a collaborative team and play a key role in shaping outcomes.

Location: Carseldine, QLD, 4034
Contract duration: 11 May 2026 - 7 May 2027 (optional extensions possible)
Flexibility: Hybrid working model

Our Client: Leading government transport agency focused on delivering high-quality infrastructure and digital solutions.

The Role:
We are seeking a proactive Business Systems Analyst to drive business requirements, functional specifications, and process improvements for key projects. You will collaborate with project teams and stakeholders to deliver solutions that align with business objectives.

Responsibilities:

Plan, document, and manage business analysis activities and stakeholder engagement.
Elicit, document, and manage business and functional requirements; ensure traceability.
Analyze current processes, identify pain points, and design optimized future-state workflows.
Document functional specifications, interface requirements, and configuration needs.
Maintain proactive communication with external implementation partners and internal teams.
Perform other duties as directed by the Project Manager and meet quarterly KPIs.

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience as a Business Systems Analyst across public and private sectors.
Strong skills in business analysis planning, requirements elicitation, and process mapping.
Ability to document As-Is and To-Be processes clearly and concisely.
Experience collaborating with project managers, IT teams, and external partners.
Knowledge of functional specifications, configuration, and interface documentation.

Benefits:

Work on high-impact projects within a leading transport agency.
Opportunity to shape business processes and contribute to digital transformation.
Flexible work environment with potential contract extensions.

How to apply:
Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). Rachel.Matthias@davidsonwp.com, reference JN -042026-42947. We are experiencing a large number of applications currently, please apply directly to the job advert, emails may be missed or not receive a reply.

Senior Finance Analyst - contract role

One of NSW leading membership organisations

Hybrid working arrangements

This leading sporting membership body is the driving force behind the sport's presence across NSW. Taking ownership of the calendar, financials, and community engagement, their presence is well known across the state. Due to excellent performance and participation in the sport, this organisation has experienced high growth and now need a temporary Senior Finance Analyst to support this growth for the coming months. Bridging the gap between finance and operations, this person will be responsible for:

Assist the corporate services teams with the production of high level financial reports

Create dashboards for senior leaders to have real time financial performance

Prepare reports for the CEO and board.

Utilise AI and BI tools to create reports for senior leadership teams

Act as a conduit between finance and operations to provide critical information and influence key decision making

Become a trusted partner to managers from different operational teams

Build and maintain financial models for business case use

To be successful, you will be CA/CPA qualified, or part qualified, and have spent a minimum of 3 years in a highly analytical role, working with financial models, creating dashboards, and presenting findings to ELT's. You will comfortable using various AI and BI tools to create financial reports and present actionable insights.

If interested, please email a copy of your CV in MS WORD to rory.garrett@lawsonelliott.com.au citing reference RG17600

Directly manage a talented Analyst

Work for and with a highly talented and well-functioning finance team

Instantly recognisable and award-winning online / tech entertainment business

Sydney CBD with hybrid working. Salary: $160K - $180K + Super + Bonus.

Company Background

Our client is a well-established, successful and instantly recognisable online entertainment brand. The company’s forward-thinking approach, coupled with its outstanding leadership team, has led them to become an undisputed market leader.

This is an enterprise that is fast-paced and dynamic with a vibrant culture.

Due to continuing growth, they are looking for a Commercial Finance Manager to join the Finance team. On offer is a professional and vibrant culture coupled with an excellent and rare hybrid working arrangement – work 3 days from home each week after training.

It’s a great place to work and grow your career in a company that promotes personal development and collaboration. Throughout the Finance team, there are numerous examples of individuals who have been promoted up through the ranks.

The Role

Your role as the Commercial Finance Manager will be broad and provide outstanding exposure across the business. You will work as part of a high-calibre FP&A team whilst reporting into a talented and energising Head of FP&A.

Your duties will broadly be broken down as follows (but not limited to):

Collaborate with stakeholders to challenge existing practices and influence decision-making to enhance profitability and operational performance.

Lead end-to-end budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning processes, working closely with cross-functional teams to deliver accurate and aligned financial plans.

Develop robust financial models, scenario analysis, and business cases to support growth initiatives, new product launches, and revenue-generating opportunities.

Evaluate product performance and margins, identifying trends, risks, and opportunities to drive revenue optimisation.

Track and report on weekly and monthly performance against forecasts, providing clear variance analysis and actionable recommendations to stakeholders.

Enhance and streamline reporting frameworks to improve efficiency, accuracy, and timeliness, including identifying opportunities for automation and advanced analytics.

Prepare high-quality financial presentations and insights for senior leadership, including CFO and Executive team reporting packs.

Lead and develop direct report/s, ensuring effective workload allocation and creating opportunities for growth and career development.

Your Background

To be considered for this Commercial Finance Manager role, you will ideally meet the following criteria:

Professionally qualified (e.g. CA, ACA, CPA, CIMA or equivalent).

Proven experience in the areas of FP&A, including budgeting, forecasting, analysis and business partnering.

Prior staff supervision experience, whether that be as a Team Leader, day-to-day Supervisor or direct Manager, would be advantageous.

Well organised with the ability to work in a fast-paced environment with competing deadlines.

Experience, enthusiasm and curiosity to improve processes and/or systems (with proven prior examples).

Have excellent communication skills and proven stakeholder management capabilities.

If you are keen to apply for this Commercial Finance Manager role, please contact Cass Pratt at Richard Lloyd on 02 8324 2014, quoting reference 9939 or click on 'apply now' below.

Please submit your CV in Word format and note that due to the high volume of responses we receive only suitable candidates will be contacted.

Business Analyst  

Insurance Technology | Platform Delivery

Not every Business Analyst role is the same.

Some are internal change roles — improving a single system over several years.

Others sit closer to delivery — where requirements become working software, and where your analysis directly shapes how a platform behaves for real clients.

This role sits firmly in the second category.

We’re partnering with a specialist insurance technology provider that designs and implements core platforms used by insurers, underwriting agencies, and broker networks.

These platforms power the way insurance products are launched, configured, and operated — from policy administration to underwriting workflows, integrations, and reporting.

They’re now looking for a Business Analyst who enjoys working close to delivery — someone who can translate complex insurance processes into clear requirements that delivery teams can actually build.

  The opportunity You will sit at the centre of delivery — working closely with Delivery Managers, Implementation Consultants, and engineers to shape how insurance platforms are implemented for clients.

This is not a documentation-heavy BA role sitting at a distance from delivery.

You’ll be directly involved in:


Understanding how insurers operate and what they’re trying to achieve


Translating those needs into clear, structured requirements


Working with implementation teams to ensure solutions behave as expected


Supporting testing and delivery through to go-live


Your work will directly influence how insurance products, underwriting rules, and workflows are implemented inside the platform.

You won’t be managing people.

You will be close to the work, influencing the solution.

  Why this role appeals to strong BAs Many experienced BAs reach a point where internal transformation programs begin to feel slow or repetitive.

This environment is different.

You’ll work across multiple implementations and client environments, solving different insurance problems rather than staying tied to a single internal system.

Delivery cycles are shorter.

Outcomes are visible.

And the work sits right at the intersection of business and technology.

You’ll be part of a delivery environment where good analysis genuinely improves how projects run — reducing ambiguity, enabling faster delivery, and helping teams build the right solutions the first time.

  What you’ll be doing You will be deeply involved in delivery:


Running stakeholder discussions and clarifying business needs


Translating requirements into user stories, functional specifications, and process flows


Working closely with Implementation Consultants who configure the platform


Bridging conversations between business stakeholders and technical teams


Supporting Agile ceremonies including stand-ups, sprint planning, and backlog refinement


Helping ensure solutions are tested properly and delivered successfully


You’ll spend less time writing documents for the sake of documentation — and more time ensuring solutions actually work for clients.

  Who this role suits This role tends to suit Business Analysts who:


Have 2-5 years of BA experience


Have worked in commercial or general insurance environments


Understand insurance processes, terminology, and stakeholder expectations


Are comfortable operating in Agile delivery teams


Have experience working in consulting-style environments or client-facing roles


Are confident engaging directly with clients — running workshops, asking the right questions, and clarifying requirements


Enjoy working closely with engineers, product teams, and delivery managers


Prefer hands-on delivery work


It particularly resonates with BAs who have worked on:


Insurance platforms or policy administration systems


Product configuration or underwriting workflows


System implementations or transformation programs


Projects where they needed to work directly with clients or external stakeholders


Just as important is mindset.

You’re someone who:


Asks the right questions


Brings structure to ambiguous problems


Is comfortable leading discussions with stakeholders


Communicates clearly with both business and technical teams


Takes pride in doing analysis properly and helping delivery teams succeed


  A final note This role isn’t designed for someone looking to step away from delivery into pure leadership.

It is a role for someone who enjoys being close to the work — shaping solutions, solving real problems, and seeing their analysis come to life in working systems.

If that sounds like you, it’s worth a closer look.

Who We Are

Entain Australia & New Zealand is fearlessly transforming the racing, sports, and entertainment landscape - reimagining how customers experience their favourite brands. As part of a global powerhouse operating in over 40 countries with nearly 30,000 people, we’re home to leading ANZ names like Ladbrokes, Neds, TAB, and Betcha. Across Entain ANZ, we’re shaping the future of sport and gaming entertainment through creative innovation, storytelling, and technology - creating experiences that connect with millions of fans.

Your Impact

As Senior Manager, Performance and Digital Media, you will lead the end-to-end function, maximising the impact and efficiency of digital media investment. You’ll drive strategy, execution, optimisation, and capability, ensuring all activity delivers against brand, performance, and commercial objectives. You will also evolve the operating model and ways of working to support scalable, high-performing delivery.

YOU will translate strategy into clear, measurable outcomes, driving acquisition, retention, and engagement through strong audience strategy, continuous testing and learning, and deeper integration with data, technology, and analytics. You will play a key role in aligning brand and performance priorities to deliver more cohesive outcomes.

This role is key to shaping how Entain connects with customers across digital channels, ensuring relevance, consistency, and efficiency at every touchpoint. You will lead and develop a high-performing team, build future-ready capability, and drive sustainable, responsible growth across the business.

A Day in the Life

Lead the Digital Media function, overseeing both Brand and Performance units to ensure delivery against brand objectives, MMM outcomes, performance targets, and broader business goals.
Design and embed new ways of working for Performance Media, including stakeholder alignment, planning rhythms, reporting frameworks, and optimisation cycles that support the business’s evolving growth and ROI targets.
Execute the overarching digital media strategy, ensuring all activity aligns to Brand, Department, and Group objectives as set by the GM and senior leadership.
Develop testing and learning agendas across all digital channels, driving continuous improvement, best-practice adoption, and innovation through structured experimentation and insight-led optimisation.
Own digital media financial governance, including forecasting, budget management, reconciliation, billing accuracy, and delivery of working media and performance investment targets.
Drive operational excellence across the Digital Media team, spanning reporting, optimisation, creative trafficking, finance, planning, and analysis, while leveraging AI and automation to scale output without incremental resource.

Your Strengths

Proven experience leading senior digital media or performance teams within complex, high-investment environments.
Deep understanding of digital media channels across brand and performance, including audience strategy, attribution, measurement, and optimisation.
Strong commercial and financial capability, including budget ownership, forecasting, and ROI optimisation.
Experience partnering with data, technology, and analytics teams to deliver scalable, insight-driven outcomes.
Comfortable operating at Senior ELT / Junior SLT level, influencing across functions and reconciling competing priorities.
A track record of leading, developing, and retaining high-performing teams through change.

Life at Entain

At Entain, it’s more than a job - it’s a place to grow, connect, and make an impact. We value individuality, celebrate collaboration, and create space for bold ideas that move people. Here, you’ll help shape the voice of some of Australia and New Zealand’s most recognised brands - while developing your own craft in a supportive, ambitious team.

Perks You’ll Love

Hybrid & Flexible: Balance your life and work.
Recognition & Rewards: Celebrate wins and enjoy retailer discounts.
Wellbeing Perks: Support for your health and happiness.
Workday Extras: Free breakfast, snacks, and onsite barista.
Office Vibes: Pool table, live sports, and fun social activities.

Entain, there’s nothing like it.

For more information, please reach out to us at careers@entaingroup.com.au or hit apply now. Due to the wagering aspect of our industry, applicants must be a minimum of 18 years to apply.

xAmplify – Leading Australia’s Digital Evolution

At xAmplify, we’re not just another technology company. We are shaping Australia’s future by combining intelligent automation and human‑centred technology services to deliver exceptional value.

Our vision is BOLD to lead Australia’s digital evolution by accelerating the safe and secure adoption of Automation and AI.

We don’t pretend to do everything. Instead, we partner with the world’s largest and most innovative technology companies, aggregating cutting‑edge solutions to transform businesses, industries, and government services. Every project we undertake is an opportunity to transform industries, solve real‑world problems, and push the boundaries of what’s possible in technology.

And as part of this growth, xAmplify became an official TechnologyOne partner following our acquisition of Green Cloud in late 2025, expanding our ERP capability across Finance, Supply Chain, Assets, HR/Payroll, Property & Rating, and more.

We are enablers. What’s ours is yours, check us out at xAmplify.com.au

About the Role

As our new Senior TechnologyOne Technical Consultant, you’ll join our expanding TechOne Practice and work with a diverse team delivering meaningful ERP modernisation and transformation projects across Australia. You’ll help clients rethink their processes, uplift critical systems, and maximise value from the TechnologyOne platform.

As a Senior Technical Consultant, you’ll represent xAmplify during requirements gathering and design phases and help structure delivery across multiple TechnologyOne modules and industry verticals.

You’ll be involved end‑to‑end across the project lifecycle, including: 

Early‑stage demonstrations and proof‑of‑concepts    
Requirements workshops and process mapping 
Solution design and documentation  
Technical configuration and development     
Data migration, integrations, testing and deployment    
Hypercare and post‑go‑live support

Responsibilities

Implementing and configuring TechnologyOne solutions to meet diverse client needs.     
Supporting uplift across modules such as Financials, Supply Chain, EAM, HRP, Corporate Performance Management, and more.
Integrating TechOne with third‑party systems using APIs, ETL tools, and web services.
Designing and maintaining BPA’s, forms, dashboards, data models, workflows, and automation.

Required Skills

At xAmplify, we look for individuals who thrive in dynamic, fast‑paced environments and bring a passion for technology, problem‑solving, and collaboration.

For this Senior TechnologyOne Technical Consultant role, we’re looking for:

3+ years of hands‑on TechnologyOne administration, development, and configuration experience across Ci or Ci Anywhere.
Experience across multiple TechnologyOne modules such as Financials, Supply Chain Management, Asset Management, Property & Rating, HR & Payroll, CPM.
Strong configuration and development capability, including:

o  XLOne reporting

o   Design Studio dashboards and data models

o   ETLand workflow automation

o   CiA Web Services (REST/SOAP)

o   BPA’s/Forms

Experience facilitating workshops, defining requirements, and producing solution design documentation in an Agile delivery model.
Ability to work collaboratively or independently, managing scope, deliverables, and client expectations.
Confidence presenting solution designs to technical and non‑technical stakeholders. 
Integration background (REST/SOAP APIs, data transformation, external system connectivity).    
Interest in mentoring junior talent and contributing to practice capability uplift.
Experience preparing effort estimates, supporting RFQ responses, and participating in technical solution conversations.  
Strong communication, organisation, and time‑management skills.

We’re flexible! If you’re in Canberra, Sydney, Brisbane, or the Sunshine Coast you’ll have the opportunity to connect with us in person in our offices, but we also welcome candidates from locations across the Australian East Coast. Our team operates on a hybrid work model, so you will have the ability to work from our office, client sites (as required with your allocated projects), or from home.

Equal Opportunity Statement

At xAmplify, we believe diversity drives innovation. We welcome applications from people of all genders, identities, ethnicities, religions, abilities, and ages. xAmplify is an advocate for diversity and inclusion, and welcomes applications from all irrespective of their gender, LGBTQI identity, ethnicity, religion, physical ability, or age.

Intrigued? Want to know more? Reach out to us at recruitment@xamplify.com.au or visit xamplify.com.au. Join us in shaping Australia’s digital landscape — because the future isn’t just coming, we’re creating it!

AI Data Governance Lead
Govern Enterprise Data for Safe AI Deployment

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid 

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search
  
Every executive wants AI. Not every executive has asked the question: "What data is Claude actually going to access, and should it?" 

When you deploy Claude into an enterprise via MCP, you're giving an AI system access to internal databases, documents, customer records, financial data, and HR files. If nobody governs what Claude can see, how data is classified, and what quality standards apply — you don't have an AI deployment. You have a liability. 

That's where you come in.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. AI Data Governance Leads are the people who make enterprise Claude deployments safe, compliant, and trustworthy — by ensuring the data foundations are solid before AI touches anything.
  
What you'll do on client engagements

Assess enterprise data landscapes before Claude deployment — classify data sensitivity, map data flows, and identify governance gaps that AI would expose or amplify
Design data access policies for MCP connections — which data Claude can access, under what conditions, with what audit trails, and for which user groups
Build data quality frameworks that ensure AI decisions are based on accurate, current, and complete information
Implement data classification schemes that determine what's safe for AI consumption — PII handling, commercial-in-confidence data, regulated data (APRA, Privacy Act, health records)
Work with Cybersecurity & AI Governance specialists to align data governance with broader AI risk frameworks
Create data governance documentation, policies, and runbooks that client teams can maintain after Galileo's engagement ends
Advise C-suite and data leadership on responsible AI data practices — translating regulatory requirements into practical governance controls
What you bring

7+ years in data governance, data management, or information management roles
Deep understanding of data classification, data quality, metadata management, and data lineage
Experience with regulatory compliance frameworks — Privacy Act, APRA CPS 234, health data regulations, or equivalent
Ability to design and implement data access controls and policies at enterprise scale
Strong stakeholder management — you can explain to a CEO why their AI project needs to slow down until data governance is in place, and make them thank you for it
Experience in regulated industries (financial services, government, healthcare) is highly valued
Bonus (not required)

Experience with data governance platforms (Collibra, Alation, Informatica, Purview)
Familiarity with AI/ML data requirements and risks
DAMA CDMP, DCAM, or equivalent data management certification
Understanding of the Australian AI Ethics Principles and their data governance implications
Experience with data sovereignty and cross-border data flow requirements
What Galileo offers

Great contract day rates reflecting the critical, senior nature of this role
You're the person who makes enterprise AI deployments defensible — when the board asks "is our AI safe?", your governance framework is the answer
Free Anthropic certification — understand exactly how Claude accesses and processes data so your governance controls are precise, not theoretical
A delivery team around you — architects, engineers, and security specialists who implement the controls you design
This role exists because AI made it necessary. Traditional data governance was about compliance and reporting. AI data governance is about controlling what a powerful autonomous system can access and do with your most sensitive information. The professionals who build expertise here are positioning themselves at the centre of the most important governance challenge in enterprise technology.
  
To apply: Share your CV and a brief overview of a data governance program you've led or contributed to — what you governed, how you approached classification and access control, and what the business impact was. Experience in regulated industries is a strong advantage.

Ad Text:
The Department of Education’s Infrastructure Services Division (ISD) manages a large and complex infrastructure portfolio supporting schools, early childhood services, and corporate facilities, and includes an annual capital and maintenance program exceeding $1.7 billion with over 2,000 projects in-flight, along with a growing and ageing educational infrastructure asset base with a replacement cost of more than $40 billion.

In response to increasing demand, growing complexity, and audit findings, ISD is implementing a range of initiatives to enhance asset management maturity, improve data quality, and strengthen decision-making capabilities.

The Asset Lifecycle Condition Assessment (ALCA) Maturity Uplift Project is a key initiative under the Asset Management Improvement (AMI) Program. One of the primary objectives is to establish a consistent, scalable, and data-driven approach to condition assessment, enabling evidence-based investment decisions and optimised asset lifecycle management.

The Business Analyst will play a critical role by delivering business analysis activities across process, data, and system domains to ensure the successful delivery of the project and its outcomes. This role sits within the Asset Management Strategy directorate, where the successful applicant will play an important role in shaping our ways of working.

As the Business Analyst - ALCA, you will collaborate with business stakeholders, technical teams, and external vendors to define requirements, improve processes, and support the procurement, design and implementation of a fit-for-purpose system solution for Asset Lifecycle Condition Assessment

Your primary focus will be on translating business needs into clear, structured requirements to support system selection, data governance uplift, and improved asset lifecycle decision-making.

Key responsibilities include:

Business Analysis & Technical Delivery
- Leading business analysis activities across process, data, and system domains

Stakeholder Engagement, Risk & Change Management
- Facilitating stakeholder workshops to capture current state processes, challenges, and future needs

Key Deliverables:
- Comprehensive current state analysis, including processes, systems, and data flows

- Functional and non-functional requirements documentation
- Data governance and data management requirements
- Procurement inputs, including requirements and evaluation criteria.
- Vendor evaluation artefacts, such as use cases and scoring frameworks
- Defined business rules and data standards
- Stakeholder engagement outputs, including workshop summaries and findings
- Contributions to implementation planning, testing, and change impact assessments

The ideal applicant will be someone who has the following:
- Strong experience delivering process, data, and system improvement initiatives
- Working in ERP environments (e.g., SAP) with a strong understanding of system constraints
- Identifying and resolving data quality issues, data flows, and integration challenges

- A proactive and outcomes-focused approach, with a commitment to delivering high-quality work
- Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and nontechnical audiences
- Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships and influence stakeholders at all levels

Qualifications

Job Title: Senior Investment Analyst - Investment Analyst ESG
Ad Text:
If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours



We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.



The Senior Investment Analyst / Investment Analyst ESG plays a key role in delivering AMP's investment and sustainability strategy. This role focuses on ESG research, investment manager assessment and stewardship, and the ongoing management of sustainable investment universes across AMP Platforms and superannuation funds. You'll help ensure ESG integration is robust, compliant and aligned to evolving member, regulatory and industry expectations.



How will you make an impact


Contribute to the delivery of AMP's sustainability strategy, including management of sustainable investment frameworks across platforms, managed portfolios and superannuation funds

Lead ESG research and analysis, combining qualitative and quantitative insights to assess investment managers, funds and ETFs

Support the selection, monitoring and oversight of ESG and sustainable investment options to achieve strong investment outcomes

Implement and enhance ESG compliance processes to meet regulatory, PRI and industry obligations

Prepare accurate and timely ESG compliance, disclosure and reporting outputs

Partner closely with Portfolio Management, Product and broader Investment teams to embed ESG considerations into investment decision-making




What you will bring to the role

Proven experience in investment research, fund manager research and/or investment consulting

Demonstrated experience across investment structures such as unit trusts, ETFs, LICs, discrete mandates and listed securities

Excellent ESG and sustainability research capability, including manager assessments, ESG data analysis and stewardship practices

Sound understanding of the regulatory and compliance environment within financial services and superannuation

Advanced analytical skills, attention to detail and technical investment knowledge and ability to manage large datasets.

Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex ESG concepts and research into clear insights

Proficiency in tools such as Morningstar Direct, MS Excel and PowerPoint; experience with Python, R or AI-enabled analysis is highly regarded



Who you are

You're a thoughtful and values led professional who understands that while technical capability matters, real impact comes from how you work with others. This is a unique role that suits someone with a investment management experience, as well as someone with solid experience who is deeply passionate about ESG and sustainable investing. You're motivated by meaningful work and take pride in contributing to outcomes that benefit members, customers and the broader community.

You bring sound judgement, adaptability and a calm, solutions focused mindset. You're genuinely curious about the ESG and investment landscape and take initiative to stay across evolving market practices, emerging thinking and industry developments. You're comfortable navigating change and ambiguity, and you're motivated to keep learning and growing in a constantly evolving field.

Above all, you value teamwork and development. You enjoy working in a collaborative, supportive environment where ideas are shared, challenge is constructive and success is collective. You're keen to build on your existing experience, deepen your knowledge and contribute thoughtfully while learning alongside others, believing that the ability to work well within a high performing team is just as critical if not more so than individual technical expertise.

To obtain a detailed Job Descrption please call Milica Radomir on 02 9257 5036



Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website.

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

AI Data & Analytics Engineer
Power Enterprise AI with Clean Data Pipelines
Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid 

Here's the dirty secret of enterprise AI: Claude is brilliant, but it's only as good as the data it can access. And most enterprise data is a mess — siloed across many systems, inconsistently formatted, poorly documented, and locked behind APIs that haven't been updated since 2018.

Before Claude can transform how a company works, someone needs to build the data pipelines that feed it. That someone is you.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. We deliver end-to-end enterprise Claude and Cowork deployments, and Data & Analytics Engineers are the people who make sure AI has the clean, reliable, well-structured data it needs to actually be useful.
  
What you'll do on client engagements

Build data pipelines that connect enterprise data sources to Claude via MCP (Model Context Protocol) — giving AI secure, structured access to the data it needs
Design and implement data transformations that make messy enterprise data usable by AI — normalisation, deduplication, enrichment, and quality validation
Work with Solutions Architects to design data architecture for Claude integrations — what data flows where, how it's cached, and how freshness is maintained
Build and maintain MCP data connectors that let Cowork query internal databases, data warehouses, and analytics platforms in real time
Implement data quality monitoring — because an AI making decisions on stale or incorrect data is worse than no AI at all
What you bring

4+ years in data engineering, analytics engineering, or similar data-focused roles
Strong SQL skills — you can write complex queries, optimise performance, and design schemas
Python proficiency — you've built data pipelines, ETL processes, or analytics tooling
Experience with modern data stack tools — dbt, Airflow, Spark, Snowflake, BigQuery, or equivalent
Understanding of data quality principles — you know that "garbage in, garbage out" is the biggest risk in enterprise AI
Comfort working with enterprise data sources — ERP exports, CRM data, financial systems, HR platforms
Bonus (not required)

Experience building data pipelines for AI/ML workloads
Familiarity with Claude API, MCP protocol, or vector databases
Cloud data platform experience (AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, GCP Dataflow)
Experience in data governance, data cataloguing, or metadata management
Certifications relevant to role
What Galileo offers

Great contract day rates depending on experience 
Free Anthropic certification — you'll understand exactly how Claude consumes data and what it needs to perform well
Work alongside Solutions Architects and Integration Engineers who handle the application layer while you own the data layer
The data engineers who learn to build AI-ready data pipelines now will be the most valuable data professionals in the market within two years. This isn't about learning a new tool — it's about becoming the person who makes enterprise AI actually work by solving the hardest problem: getting the data right.

To apply: Share your CV and a brief description of the most complex data pipeline or data platform you've built — what it connected, how you ensured quality, and what consumed the output. Bonus points if you've worked with AI/ML data requirements.

Company description:

Job description:



The Principal Energy Market Analyst in the Energy Market Intelligence Unit (EMIU) prepares relevant quantitative analysis and modelling to meet the needs of the organisation. This role leads quantitative analysis on energy markets and undertakes energy market modelling using optimisation tools. The position requires a detailed understanding of energy markets. The Principal Market Analyst is responsible for applying and integrating relevant mathematical and statistical processes, using a variety of software, to build electricity, gas and stochastic simulation models. The Principal Market Modelling Analyst is responsible for applying and integrating relevant mathematical and statistical processes, using a variety of software, to build electricity, gas and stochastic simulation models. The position is also responsible for communicating quantitative assumptions, methodologies and insights in clear and concrete terms.

Specialist/Technical Expertise/Qualifications

Bachelor, honours, or advanced degree such as data science, statistics, mathematics or other highly quantitative degree
Demonstrated in-depth understanding of energy market dynamics
High-level familiarity with energy market modelling software such PLEXOS or Prophet
Expert ability in a programming language used in the development of quantitative analysis, machine learning models and energy market models (e.g., R or Python)

Job Title: Specialist Data Management | Perth | Mon - Fri
Ad Text:
BHP is a world-leading resources company. We work in more than 90 locations worldwide and our products are sold globally.

We've positioned our business to support the megatrends shaping our world. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. Copper for renewable energy. And we are moving into potash to support more sustainable farming.

A resource mix for today - and critical to the future.



About the Role

The Geoscience Systems and Data Management team is seeking a motivated and collaborative Specialist Data Management professional to join WAIO Geoscience.

Come and be a part of a diverse, inclusive, and technical team working towards our purpose of "delivering orebody knowledge to realise our full potential". We enable high‑quality, trusted and accessible geoscience data to characterise Western Australian Iron Ore (WAIO) mineral inventory across both strategic and tactical time horizons for our customers. The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring the quality, integrity, security, and availability of WAIO Geoscience data, supporting Geoscientists to extract maximum value from drilling and field data.

Reporting to the Superintendent Geoscience Systems and Data Management, this G10 role forms part of a highly experienced team of geoscience data and systems specialists.

You will be responsible for managing WAIO's critical geoscience datasets including drillhole and field mapping data across exploration, tactical drilling, RCGC, geotechnical, hydrogeological, geometallurgical and closure planning programs and enabling their effective integration into downstream modelling, evaluation, and decision‑making processes.

This role offers the opportunity to deepen your technical expertise across geoscience systems, SQL‑based data platforms, data governance, automation, and cloud technologies, while contributing to continuous improvement and change initiatives across the broader Resource Characterisation function.

About the role

As a key member of the Geoscience Systems and Data Management team within WAIO | Resource Characterisation, you will:

Administer and maintain Geosciences' centralised drilling database, ensuring system reliability, performance, and availability
Perform data loading, validation, transformation, and integrity checks across the drillhole data lifecycle
Manage and support Geoscience data quality processes in alignment with defined data standards and Data Steward requirements
Provide subject matter expertise and support to Geoscience teams for the planning, collection, management and use of drilling and field mapping data
Deliver help‑desk support and advanced troubleshooting for Geoscience systems and data management issues
Develop, maintain, and optimise SQL queries, stored procedures and database objects to support reporting, analytics, and operational workflows
Contribute to Geoscience systems and data initiatives, including platform upgrades, process improvements, and automation opportunities
Support the design and improvement of data governance, documentation, and operational procedures
Identify and implement opportunities to improve efficiency, scalability and data value through automation and database optimisation across on‑premise and cloud environments
Engage and Collaborate with Geoscience and Technology business stakeholders to deliver practical, fit‑for‑purpose data solutions aligned to business strategy
Incorporate product ownership and project management towards Geosciences' centralised drilling database evolving requirements
About You

We're looking for someone who brings a balance of strong technical capability, geoscience domain understanding and customer focus, with a passion for high-quality data and its potential applications. You will ideally have:

Qualifications & Experience

A degree in either Geoscience, Information Management, Computer Science, or a related discipline
Minimum 5 years' experience in a similar data management or database administration role
Experience in exploration and/or mining industry, particularly geoscience or drilling data, is highly regarded

Technical Capability

Design and build database structures, tables, views, stored procedures, optimised for performance and scalability
Hands‑on experience administering databases using Microsoft SQL Server, proficiency in Transact-SQL (T-SQL) is highly desirable
Experience with geological drilling database systems (e.g. Geobank, Acquire)
Exposure to SQL‑based reporting and analytics, such as SSRS and/or Power BI
Working knowledge of cloud or hybrid data platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks) is highly desirable
Experience with SQL and/or Python based application development or data‑driven tools is a strong advantage
Basic to intermediate scripting or programming skills (e.g. Python) are beneficial

Personal Attributes

Passionate about data quality, integrity and enabling data‑driven decision‑making
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and attention‑to‑detail skills
A clear, inclusive communicator who values collaboration and customer outcomes
Proactive mindset with a continuous improvement and automation‑first approach
Comfortable working in environments undergoing change and system evolution

AI Change & Adoption Manager
Drive Enterprise Claude Adoption

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid 

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Here's the truth about enterprise AI rollouts: the technology works. The people problem is what kills them.

Companies are deploying Claude and Cowork across their organisations, and the technology is genuinely transformative. But if employees don't adopt it, if managers resist it, if teams don't understand how it changes their workflows,  the project fails. Every time.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. We deliver end-to-end Claude deployments for enterprise clients, and we need Change & Adoption Managers who understand that technology transformation is really a people transformation.

What you'll do on client engagements
Design and execute adoption strategies for Claude and Cowork deployments across organisations of 500–10,000+ employees
Run hands-on workshops showing teams how AI changes their specific workflows — not generic AI demos, but "here's how Claude handles your actual invoice processing"
Build adoption measurement frameworks — tracking usage, sentiment, productivity impact, and resistance patterns
Coach executives and middle management on leading through AI-driven change — the layer where adoption most often stalls
Create communication plans that address the real fears (job displacement, skill obsolescence) honestly and constructively
Work with Integration Engineers and Config Specialists to ensure the technology experience matches the change narrative
What you bring
5+ years in change management, digital transformation, or organisational development
Experience leading adoption programs for enterprise technology rollouts — ERP, CRM, Office, collaboration platforms, or similar
Strong facilitation and workshop skills — you can hold a room of skeptical employees and bring them along
Data-driven approach to measuring adoption — you track metrics, not just vibes
Understanding that AI adoption is different from traditional IT rollouts — the emotional and identity dimensions are bigger
Bonus (not required)
Prosci, ADKAR, or similar change management certification
Experience with AI or automation adoption specifically
Background in L&D, instructional design, or training program development
Experience in regulated industries where change moves slowly and stakeholder management is critical
What Galileo offers
Great contract rates depending on experience
Free Anthropic certification — you'll understand the technology deeply enough to credibly lead adoption
A delivery team around you — architects design the solution, engineers build it, you make sure humans actually use it
Growing client pipeline across industries that are all hitting the same adoption challenges
Why this role matters more than any other on the team:
An Integration Engineer can build a perfect Claude deployment. A Solutions Architect can design flawless architecture. But if 4,000 employees ignore it and keep using their old workflows, the entire engagement was a waste of money. You're the person who makes the investment pay off.

If you've ever watched a brilliant technology project fail because nobody managed the human side — this is your chance to fix that at scale, with the most transformative technology since the internet.

To apply: Share your CV and a brief example of an adoption program you led — what worked, what didn't, and what you'd do differently. We value honesty over perfection.

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Data Engineer with a proven track record in designing, building and maintaining core data platforms and pipelines that will underpin regulatory insight, intelligence and decision-making.

Reporting to the Senior Manager Data Management and Platform, this role will focus on ensuring that the data collected, integrated and stored by ONRSR is reliable, secure, scalable and fit-for-purpose in the safety regulation context. In this newly formed role, you will have the opportunity to meaningfully contribute to a new, strategic operating model where data is key to regulatory decisions and outcomes. With strong relationships across the Safety and Regulatory Insights team, as well as other stakeholders, you will effectively translate regulatory and operational requirements into robust technical solutions that enable evidence-based regulation and safety improvement.

As the only organisation in Australia with a complete view of national rail safety performance, ONRSR is uniquely placed to provide safety insights. Leveraging your strengths and unique skillset, combined with your values-led approach to working and producing outcomes, the Data Engineer is responsible for ensuring reliability, availability and performance of data platforms supporting the enterprise data warehouse and advanced analytics use cases. You will apply a continuous improvement mindset, optimising data structures and performance to support analytics, reporting and intelligence.

If you are passionate about the transformative ability of data, information and insights while looking to have an impact on rail safety, this may be the role for you.

About you – your impact and contribution

In this role, you will translate complex regulatory and operational requirements into practical, scalable data solutions within a modern Microsoft Fabric environment, while taking ownership of data pipelines and platform components to ensure reliability, performance and maintainability. You will work closely with analysts and stakeholders to deliver data that is usable, trusted and well understood, leveraging Fabric capabilities to enable seamless data integration, modelling and access. You will also contribute to improving how data is structured, governed and consumed across the organisation, with a continuous focus on simplifying, optimising and modernising the data ecosystem.

Key to success in this role includes:  

Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.  
Drive for, and skills to, building meaningful and effective relationships across the organisation.  
A commitment to continuous improvement, including own professional development as well as ways of working, contributing and producing outcomes for the organisation.  
Demonstrated experience in data engineering, including data pipelines, databases and integration technologies.  
A constructive approach to design solutions in complex, multi-source data environments.  
Understanding the importance of information security, privacy and risk considerations in government or regulated settings, including relevant governance obligations.
Experience using Apache Parquet and MS Fabric, as well as advanced knowledge and experience of SQL and working with heavily normalised data structures.
A strategic thinking mindset, combined with a strong ability to prioritise competing demands through understanding the role of data in various analytical models.    

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes.

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Data Engineer with a proven track record in designing, building and maintaining core data platforms and pipelines that will underpin regulatory insight, intelligence and decision-making.

Reporting to the Senior Manager Data Management and Platform, this role will focus on ensuring that the data collected, integrated and stored by ONRSR is reliable, secure, scalable and fit-for-purpose in the safety regulation context. In this newly formed role, you will have the opportunity to meaningfully contribute to a new, strategic operating model where data is key to regulatory decisions and outcomes. With strong relationships across the Safety and Regulatory Insights team, as well as other stakeholders, you will effectively translate regulatory and operational requirements into robust technical solutions that enable evidence-based regulation and safety improvement.

As the only organisation in Australia with a complete view of national rail safety performance, ONRSR is uniquely placed to provide safety insights. Leveraging your strengths and unique skillset, combined with your values-led approach to working and producing outcomes, the Data Engineer is responsible for ensuring reliability, availability and performance of data platforms supporting the enterprise data warehouse and advanced analytics use cases. You will apply a continuous improvement mindset, optimising data structures and performance to support analytics, reporting and intelligence.

If you are passionate about the transformative ability of data, information and insights while looking to have an impact on rail safety, this may be the role for you.

About you – your impact and contribution

In this role, you will translate complex regulatory and operational requirements into practical, scalable data solutions within a modern Microsoft Fabric environment, while taking ownership of data pipelines and platform components to ensure reliability, performance and maintainability. You will work closely with analysts and stakeholders to deliver data that is usable, trusted and well understood, leveraging Fabric capabilities to enable seamless data integration, modelling and access. You will also contribute to improving how data is structured, governed and consumed across the organisation, with a continuous focus on simplifying, optimising and modernising the data ecosystem.

Key to success in this role includes:  

Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.  
Drive for, and skills to, building meaningful and effective relationships across the organisation.  
A commitment to continuous improvement, including own professional development as well as ways of working, contributing and producing outcomes for the organisation. 
Demonstrated experience in data engineering, including data pipelines, databases and integration technologies.  
A constructive approach to design solutions in complex, multi-source data environments.  
Understanding the importance of information security, privacy and risk considerations in government or regulated settings, including relevant governance obligations.
Experience using Apache Parquet and MS Fabric, as well as advanced knowledge and experience of SQL and working with heavily normalised data structures.
A strategic thinking mindset, combined with a strong ability to prioritise competing demands through understanding the role of data in various analytical models.    

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes.

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

Enquiries to: ONRSR’s People and Capability team by emailing recruitment@onrsr.com.au 

Salary: $142,416 - $168,216 + 12% super

Applications close: Wednesday, 22 April 2026, 6:00PM

At ONRSR, we’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, including those of diverse gender identities, sexual orientations, cultures, religions, ages, abilities and neurodiversity.

If there’s anything we can do to make the recruitment process more accessible or comfortable for you, please let us know – we’re here to support you.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

NOTE:

You must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident or hold a valid work permit or visa, as we are unable to provide sponsorship.
Applications are sought from direct candidates only. No agencies please.
Application closing dates are at the discretion of ONRSR and may change without notice.
