Position Description - Data Engineer

Location: Brisbane, QLD

Salary Package: $130-$150K + Super & Bonus DOE

Role highlights: Strategic Pricing Manager role in a global business



Role Overview

Exclusive to Reo Group, this is a highly exciting role to provide ongoing support to the Head of Pricing and key stakeholders within a global Retailer. This role is being recruited to optimise growth and commercial opportunity and works within an entrepreneurial environment whereby positive change and transformation are the norm.

Where the client is looking to achieve value in this hire is through acquiring a strategic individual who has demonstrated prior work in driving business growth, specifically around pricing, innovation, and gaining stakeholder trust. The BAU aspect to this role is also very varied and requires a candidate who is happy to work with a high degree of autonomy. Key technical requirements involve prior use of Snowflake and either Power BI / Advanced MS Excel.



Responsibilities

Design, test and implement Pricing and Promotional strategies that balance volume with profitability
Monitor food and labour costs as well as competitor pricing and demand trends
Partner with senior Marketing stakeholders to design and optimise promotional campaigns.
Conduct cost-benefit analysis to ensure promotions are commercially viable and aligned with brand positioning
Ensure pricing strategies are executable across various channels
Develop reports and dashboards to monitor pricing impact alongside margin and benefit realisation



Candidate Profile

This role is a brilliant opportunity for a candidate with solid exposure to strategic pricing initiatives, the ability to provide first class business partnering expertise and someone who looks to bring clarity in a positively changing business. The client needs someone who is invested in building a career in a business which is fast paced and where you will be required to demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to ever changing scenarios.

The ideal candidate will ideally be experienced within a B2C organisation with strong communication skills and the ability to utilise tools such as SQL, Snowflake and Power BI / MS Excel. The role could provide an opportunity for a candidate to step-up from a Senior Pricing Analyst role or step across from an existing Pricing Manager position.



Company, Culture & Benefits

The business is an innovative global giant and this role will be part of a team that supports business growth. This organisation is known for being people focused and prides itself on being committed to serving the consumer products industry across the globe. A hybrid working pattern is available for this role, with the team being office based for 4 days per week.



Pricing Manager role to drive strategy and change
Be rewarded: $130-$150k + Super & Bonus DOE
Opportunity to support acceleration of Business change



Apply

If this sounds like you, please send your resume by clicking the "apply" link below. The reference number for this role is LFPM65166. Please allow for 3 - 5 working days for us to process all the applications. All applicants will receive feedback from us, either via email or a telephone call.

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity 

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to drive and enable our Safety Insights and Regulatory Intelligence capability through the design, development and maintenance of high-quality insights and analytics. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Advanced Analytics and Insights in this newly formed role, you will be a key contributor to a refined, strategic operating model where transforming data into meaningful insights, intelligence and visual analytics products will play an integral role in supporting regulatory decision-making. 

Collaborating with stakeholders across the organisation, you will elicit business requirements to effectively translate them into clear, reliable and decision-ready insights and intelligence solutions for executives, regulators and operational teams. Your passion for meaningful data insights will drive you to create trusted, consistent and accessible business intelligence products that will assist in risk-based regulatory planning, monitoring of safety performance and regulatory outcomes, as well as the transparency and defensibility of regulatory decisions.  

If you are an experienced business intelligence professional, looking to make a positive impact in a purpose-driven and values-based organisation that is focused on improving community safety, this may be the opportunity for you. 

About you – your impact and contribution

We are seeking a motivated, diligent and experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to play a pivotal role in advancing our regulatory intelligence capability. You will contribute to building a modern insights-driven regulatory environment where data is transformed into clear defensible and actionable insights that informs national safety decisions.  

To be successful in the role, you will be a proactive team player, collaborating with a wide range of stakeholders, and driven by producing meaningful business intelligence services and outcomes. You will have proven experience in designing, developing and maintaining high quality and impactful dashboards, reports and visualisations that provide deducible insights for their intended audiences. Your deep technical expertise will support the application of best practice data visualisation, presenting complex information clearly and accurately. 

As the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, interpersonal skills are essential, leveraging expert communication skills, rapport and relationship-building as well as coaching skills to support the seamless integration of data services across the organisation as well as data literacy uplift. 

The ideal candidate for this role will have: 

Demonstrated analytical, and problem-solving skills, including a well-developed ability to translate complex data into recommendations and commentary to inform decision making.
Proven experience in defining semantic layers, metrics, dimensions and business rules.
Certification in Microsoft Power BI, MS Fabric or other relevant BI certifications.
Proficiency in SQL and working with structured data models in data warehouses.
Deep understanding of the existing and emerging tools and technology in the field.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.
A commitment to continuous improvement and growth, including own professional development as well as the ability to coach and support other team members.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and integrity.
A collaborative mindset paired with the ability to work within multidisciplinary teams.
Demonstrated experience in understanding and applying data governance principles, including data standards, metadata, lineage and stewardship.
Experience working with sensitive or regulated data, including ensuring its integrity and appropriate use. 

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People 

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes. 

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

Position Description - Senior Business Intelligence Analyst

About the role – Multiple roles (Permanent & 12-month fixed term contract)

Calling for Payment System integration and Digital Banking end to end solution delivery experience for Sr. Business analyst roles.

It’s an exciting time to be a Senior Business Analyst at People First Bank!  Our team of Business Analysts are at the forefront of shaping the creation of a new bank by working on exciting and complex projects.

We are now offering a unique opportunity for an outcome-driven Senior Business Analyst with strong technical experience across the Payments System integration & Compliance projects, Financial Crimes working in a cross-functional team to deliver digital banking solutions and supporting problem solving activities that promote customer centric intuitive solutions. You will collaborate with multiple stakeholders to provide end-to-end ownership of requirements and user stories whilst coordinating and supporting problem solving activities that promote customer centric intuitive solutions. You will work collaboratively with business representatives, delivery lead, product owners, and technology leads to add value and drive outcomes across the business. Furthermore, as a Business Analyst, you will actively contribute to the Community of Practice maturity across the team, helping to shape best practices and guide team members.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Lead payments domain analysis, partner with Product Owners and Delivery Leads to ensure solutions deliver measurable customer and business outcomes

·        Collaborating with design and frontline teams to understand problems our customers are experiencing, apply design thinking principles to create user-centric and effective solutions.

·        Lead end-to-end discovery and delivery for complex initiatives, partnering with stakeholders to shape, define, and deliver solutions aligned to business strategy and technical feasibility.

·        Own delivery scoping and requirements definition for complex initiatives, leading story development, customer journeys, and process changes to clearly articulate outcomes, impacts, and rationale for change.

·        Own backlog quality through effective refinement, testable acceptance criteria, and alignment to delivery priorities, and readiness for delivery.

·        Proactively identify, assess, and manage risks, dependencies, and cross‑functional impacts, providing clear recommendations to support decision‑making across business and technology.

·        Support and influence key delivery decisions by evaluating options, trade‑offs, regulatory impacts, and technical constraints.

·        Champion legal and compliance obligations requirements applicable, where required engage with business owner to ensure compliance is met.

·        Key driver to the planning and support business readiness activities.

·        Define requirements with the business with designing reporting and dashboards that will feed to downstream data and technology teams.

·        Proactively manage stakeholder alignment and resolve competing priorities across business and technology.

·        Define functional and non-functional requirements, ensuring regulatory traceability and downstream data impacts are considered.

·        Support change, operational readiness, and smooth transition of solutions into BAU

·        Be an active member of the Business Analyst practice to drive improvements in the Business Analyst capability

·        Support the design, implementation, and embedding of development programs for BA cohort.

·        Actively promote continuous improvements to ways of working across the business.




 About you 

You are a Senior Business Analyst with solid experience delivering payments related initiatives. Adept at bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions through effective stakeholder engagement, structured analysis and a collaborative approach. You enjoy guiding and sharing knowledge with others and work well with a large team to achieve great outcomes for our customers.

To be successful in the role, you must have the following skills:

·        A seasoned BA with 8+ experience in all facets of business analysis.

·        Strong knowledge of digital banking products and services, such as onboarding, account origination, payments, lending and digital channels

·        Strong confidence in leading payments and cards solution, technical experience to derive requirements from frontend digital banking solutions to backend systems including payment orchestration layer APIs (e.g. payment gateways, processors, acquirers)

·        Applying your knowledge of Australian banking regulations to satisfy our regulatory obligations within Australian Payments, Cards and Fraud compliance including payment and card scheme requirements

·        The ability to break down complex problems and identify solutions

·        Understanding of API's and experience in creating high level solution designs

·        Experience with the Backbase Digital Banking Platform is highly regarded.

·        In-depth understanding of IT Risk frameworks.




Additionally, it’s essential that you have experience with the following:

Financial services industry experience 

Technical interface specification development

Customer centric product development

Process analysis and documentation

Experience working with customer relationship management software, especially Salesforce CRM, would be highly regarded

Be an engaging communicator, comfortable negotiating and influencing your key stakeholder management group.

Adaptable and capable in dealing with complexities and ambiguity in a constantly evolving environment.

Experience managing multiple activities and issues across numerous projects in a high paced environment.

Experience in requirements management, utilizing Agile methodologies and tools including Jira and Confluence. 

Enjoys leading and coaching other BAs.

Certification such as BABOK, Lean Six Sigma

 

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are across Adelaide, Brisbane and Toowoomba, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 

A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.
